message:
🌱 Add alertmanager: PagerDuty and structured alerts data mission (#1719)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "alertmanager-3218-pagerduty-and-structured-alerts-data",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "alertmanager: PagerDuty and structured alerts data",
+    "description": "PagerDuty and structured alerts data. Requested by 38+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current alertmanager deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nhelm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working alertmanager installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review alertmanager configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nkubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager\n```\nI feel there is a need for a new PagerDuty integration in the Alertmanager which would keep alerts data in a structured form when transmitted to PagerDuty as opposed to the currently templated form."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for PagerDuty and structured alerts data",
+        "description": "This change allows nested key/value pair in pageduty configuration.\n\nEvents API V1 supports an object in `details` field:\nhttps://developer.pagerduty.com/docs/send-v1-event#parameters\n\nEvents API V2 supports an object in `payload.custom_details`\n```yaml\n\"custom_details\": {\n  \"firing\":       `{{ template \"pagerduty.default.instances\" .Alerts.Firing }}`,\n  \"resolved\":     `{{ template \"pagerduty.default.instances\" .Alerts.Resolved }}`,\n  \"num_firing\":   `{{ .Alerts.Firing | len }}`,\n  \"num_resolved\": `{{ .Alerts.Resolved | len }}`\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nkubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"PagerDuty and structured alerts data\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This change allows nested key/value pair in pageduty configuration.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "\"custom_details\": {\n  \"firing\":       `{{ template \"pagerduty.default.instances\" .Alerts.Firing }}`,\n  \"resolved\":     `{{ template \"pagerduty.default.instances\" .Alerts.Resolved }}`,\n  \"num_firing\":   `{{ .Alerts.Firing | len }}`,\n  \"num_resolved\": `{{ .Alerts.Resolved | len }}`\n}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "alertmanager",
+      "graduated",
+      "observability",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "alertmanager"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/issues/3218",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/pull/3944"
+    },
+    "reactions": 38,
+    "comments": 2,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-24T06:29:57.115Z",

+}
